2026-04-20T17:36:54.9775686Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T17:36:54.9776259Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T17:36:54.9776698Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T17:36:54.9777063Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T17:36:54.9777378Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T17:36:54.9777619Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T17:36:54.9777779Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T17:36:54.9777959Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T17:36:54.9778131Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T17:36:54.9778360Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-20T17:36:54.9778857Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T17:36:54.9779117Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}

2026-04-20T17:40:54.7853882Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T17:37:58.527327695Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58389,\"title\":\"[SdkAnalyzers] Docs: CHANGELOG missing AZC0101 entry and README missing standard sections\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.SdkAnalyzers`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit f74b0b25c (PR #58154) by `@m-redding`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58154 added:\\n- **AZC0101** analyzer: detects `ConfigureAwait(true)` in Azure SDK library code and warns to use `ConfigureAwait(false)` instead\\n- **ConfigureAwaitCodeFixProvider**: automatic code fixer that replaces `ConfigureAwait(true)` → `ConfigureAwait(false)`\\n- Shared async infrastructure: `AsyncPatternAnalyzer`, `MethodBodyAnalyzer`, `AsyncAnalyzerUtilities`\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG.md** lists only `AZC0012` in Features Added — does not mention the newly-added `AZC0101` rule, its code fixer, or the shared async pattern utilities introduced in this push\\n- **README.md** is missing the required top-level sections: *Getting started* (install, prerequisites, authenticate), *Key concepts*, *Examples*, *Troubleshooting*, *Next steps*, and *Contributing*\\n- **README.md** does not describe how library authors can suppress AZC0101 (or other rules), nor does it provide a quick usage example showing what the analyzer flags\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### 1a. `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/CHANGELOG.md` — edit\\n\\nAdd an entry for AZC0101 and the code fixer under `## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` → `### Features Added`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Initial release of Azure.SdkAnalyzers\\n- Added AZC0012: Avoid single word type names analyzer\\n- Added AZC0020: Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext analyzer\\n- Added AZC0101: Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` analyzer with automatic code fix (replaces `true` with `false`)\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/README.md` — edit\\n\\nReplace the current README content with a fully structured document matching the canonical template. The README currently has a valid intro and rules table; keep those and add the missing sections around them:\\n\\n```markdown\\n# Azure SDK Analyzers\\n\\nRoslyn analyzers that enforce [Azure SDK Design Guidelines]((azure.github.io/redacted) for .NET library authors.\\n\\nThis package is automatically included in all Azure SDK libraries in this repository to ensure consistent code quality and adherence to Azure SDK conventions.\\n\\n## Getting started\\n\\n### Install the package\\n\\nInstall the `Azure.SdkAnalyzers` NuGet package via the .NET CLI:\\n\\n```\\ndotnet add package Azure.SdkAnalyzers\\n```\\n\\nOr via the NuGet Package Manager:\\n\\n```\\nInstall-Package Azure.SdkAnalyzers\\n```\\n\\n### Prerequisites\\n\\n- .NET SDK 6.0 or later\\n- A project that targets any of the supported Azure SDK target frameworks\\n\\n### Authenticate the client\\n\\nThis package contains build-time analyzers only. No authentication is required.\\n\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.SdkAnalyzers` provides Roslyn (compile-time) analyzers and code fixers that enforce patterns required by the [Azure SDK Design Guidelines for .NET]((azure.github.io/redacted) Diagnostics fire as warnings during `dotnet build` and are visible in the IDE editor.\\n\\nFor the full list of rules, see [docs/list-of-diagnostics.md](docs/list-of-diagnostics.md).\\n\\n## Implemented Rules\\n\\n| Rule | Description | Fix |\\n|------|-------------|-----|\\n| **AZC0012** | Avoid single word type names | — |\\n| **AZC0020** | Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext | — |\\n| **AZC0101** | Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` | ✅ |\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n### AZC0101 — ConfigureAwait(false)\\n\\nThe analyzer flags any `await` that passes `true` to `ConfigureAwait`:\\n\\n```\\n// ❌ AZC0101 warning — ConfigureAwait(true) is almost always wrong in library code\\nawait task.ConfigureAwait(true);\\n\\n// ✅ Correct — use false to avoid capturing the caller\\u0026#39;s SynchronizationContext\\nawait task.ConfigureAwait(false);\\n```\\n\\nA lightbulb code fix **\\u0026#34;Use ConfigureAwait(false)\\u0026#34;** is available in Visual Studio and VS Code to perform the replacement automatically.\\n\\n### AZC0012 — Avoid single-word type names\\n\\n```\\n// ❌ AZC0012 warning\\npublic class Manager { }\\n\\n// ✅ Use a descriptive multi-word name\\npublic class TableManager { }\\n```\\n\\n### AZC0020 — Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext\\n\\n```\\n// ❌ AZC0020 warning — token is dropped\\nawait client.UpdateAsync(content, new RequestContext());\\n\\n// ✅ Propagate the token\\nawait client.UpdateAsync(content, new RequestContext { CancellationToken = cancellationToken });\\n```\\n\\n## Troubleshooting\\n\\n### Suppressing a diagnostic\\n\\nMost rules can be suppressed with the standard `#pragma warning disable` / `#pragma warning restore` syntax or via a `[SuppressMessage]` attribute if the diagnostic is not configured as non-suppressible:\\n\\n```\\n#pragma warning disable AZC0012 // Justified: intentionally generic name used here\\npublic class Format { }\\n#pragma warning restore AZC0012\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** AZC0101 is a **Warning** and can be suppressed. In the rare case where `ConfigureAwait(true)` is intentional, prefer omitting the `ConfigureAwait` call entirely.\\n\\n### Build output shows no diagnostics\\n\\nEnsure the package is referenced in your `.csproj` and that the project targets a framework compatible with Roslyn analyzers (net6.0 or later is recommended).\\n\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Review the [Azure SDK Design Guidelines for .NET]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n- See [docs/list-of-diagnostics.md](docs/list-of-diagnostics.md) for detailed per-rule documentation\\n- Browse [AZC0101.md](docs/AZC0101.md) for detailed information on the ConfigureAwait rule\\n\\n## Contributing\\n\\nSee [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for contribution guidelines.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo test-backed snippets are required for this analyzer package — the examples above use plain `csharp` fences since there are no live client calls to exercise. If a snippet placeholder like `` ```C# Snippet:AZC0101Example `` is added to the README, a corresponding test file must be created:\\n\\n- **Test file path:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/tests/Azure.SdkAnalyzers.Tests/Samples/Sample_AZC0101.cs`\\n- Wrap sample code in `#region Snippet:AZC0101Example` / `#endregion`\\n- Use `[Test]` (NUnit) on the method\\n- Wrap test-only assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`\\n\\nHowever, since these examples are of analyzer violations (not runnable code), it is acceptable to use plain `csharp` fences without snippet placeholders.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README must contain these sections in order. After applying Step 1b above, all are present:\\n\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 tools` — only needed if snippet placeholders were added to README\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 tools` — only needed if public types/members were added or changed\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24680729926/agentic_workflow) · ● 592K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58389\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T17:35:23Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T17:35:23Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
